description: Model output is JSON.parsed and consumed directly, so malformed, truncated, or wrong-typed structure flows into downstream code.
body: Failure mode: the code calls JSON.parse on the response and uses the result without validating it against a schema, so a truncated payload, a prose-wrapped object, or a wrong-typed field is consumed silently. Detection signals: parse with no schema validation; no check of the length finish-reason; coercion or field drops to force a parse; no conformance run. Do not regex-extract JSON and coerce it to fit. Safer fallback: validate every response against a strict schema, detect truncation via the finish-reason, run a bounded repair loop, and return a typed error that blocks the downstream action when repair fails.
